overview: The PRIP Scheme (Scheme for Promotion of Research and Innovation in Pharma MedTech Sector) is a Central Sector Scheme under the Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India. It aims to promote research and innovation in the pharmaceutical and medical technology sectors by providing financial assistance to industry and academia for strategic priority innovation projects, centres of excellence, and industry-academia collaboration. The scheme supports activities such as in-licensing of intellectual property, research and development, and commercialization of institutional intellectual property, with a focus on areas of public health concern where market potential is relatively lower.
objectives: Establish Centres of Excellence (CoEs) at NIPERs to build specific research capacities in identified priority areas through collaboration with industry partners

Support Strategic Priority Innovation (SPI) projects in areas of India's public health concern with relatively lower market potential, such as neglected tropical diseases, rare diseases, antimicrobial resistance, outbreak/pandemic pathogens, and vaccine-preventable diseases

Facilitate industry-academia collaboration for in-licensing of intellectual property from reputed government academic and research institutions to develop, translate, and commercialize institutional intellectual property
Strengthen industry-academia linkage and promote joint research and development activities
Ensure prudent fiscal management and effective resource utilization aligned with the scheme's objectives
Monitor implementation of approved proposals and research programmes through the Steering Committee
Consider and decide requests for modification in the research programme and associated deliverables for Centres of Excellence
Review implementation of approved proposals and associated deliverables finalised by the committee
eligibility: Every entity registered in India that is a company registered under the Companies Act, 2013 or under any previous company law (excluding one-person companies, companies with unlimited liability, dividend-paying companies, or companies whose objects include promotion of commerce, art, science, sports, education, research, social welfare, religion, charity, or environmental protection, or whose profits may only be applied for promoting said objects), a Limited Liability Partnership registered under the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008, or a partnership firm other than a sole proprietorship firm registered under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932. For Early Stage Projects, the applicant must be a startup with a valid certificate from the Inter-Ministerial Board of Certification under DPIIT notification G.S.R. 127(E) dated 19.2.2019, or an MSME with a valid Udyam Registration Certificate under MSME Ministry notification S.O. 2119(E) dated 26.6.2020. Applicants are ineligible if any key managerial personnel is convicted of an offence involving moral turpitude or corruption and sentenced to imprisonment for six months or more, or if the applicant or its holding company has filed for insolvency, winding-up, liquidation, or resolution proceedings.
benefits: Financial assistance for Centres of Excellence at NIPERs, Strategic Priority Innovation projects, and industry-academia collaboration for in-licensing of intellectual property. The scheme provides grant-in-aid for research and development, infrastructure development, equipment procurement, and other eligible activities. Benefits include support for building research capacities, translating and commercializing institutional intellectual property, augmenting institutional research capacities, and promoting industry-academia linkage. Financial assistance is disbursed into a project-specific dedicated account in a Scheduled Commercial Bank, subject to inspection and special audit, including forensic audit, by the Central Government. The scheme also provides for benefit-sharing mechanisms such as share allotment or revenue sharing, and supports milestone-linked financial assistance disbursement in four instalments, with the fourth instalment being at least 15% of the approved total financial assistance on a reimbursement basis.
financial_support: Financial assistance is provided as grant-in-aid disbursed in four instalments, with the fourth instalment being at least 15% of the approved total project cost and disbursed on a reimbursement basis. The Central Government may consider the project plan with or without modifications and accord in-principle approval. Upon acceptance, the applicant must submit documents evidencing financial closure for the approved total project cost within 60 days, execute a duly signed funding agreement with the approved project plan annexed, and, if opting for benefit-share via share allotment, execute a duly signed share allotment agreement. Co-funding is required to the extent that total co-funding equals the approved total project cost less the approved total financial assistance, and the proportion of total co-funding to approved total financial assistance must be maintained at each instalment disbursement stage. The beneficiary must ensure cumulative co-funding in the required proportion is credited into the project account before each disbursement. Disbursement of milestone-linked financial assistance is based on achievement of milestones as per corresponding criteria, and instalments may be utilised for the corresponding purposes as specified in the project plan.
application_process: 1. The applicant submits the application as per the application form prescribed in Appendix K through the online portal maintained by the Project Management Agency (PMA). 2. The PMA conducts an examination as per the checklist in Appendix I within 15 working days of receipt of the application or any subsequent submission of a revised application if the original was returned as incomplete. 3. If the application is found to be incomplete, the PMA informs the applicant accordingly, and the applicant must complete the incomplete application within 15 days of such communication, failing which the application will be closed. 4. A non-refundable application fee, as mentioned in Appendix C, is payable electronically for each application. 5. All applications are submitted through the online portal maintained by the PMA; if the portal is not available, applications may be submitted in physical form to the PMA. 6. The applicant submits an undertaking in the format given in Appendix E and also an undertaking in the format given in Appendix G, consenting to audit of their manufacturing site/offices for verification of information/data submitted along with the application. 7. As part of the application, the applicant submits a plan based on sequential milestones to be achieved, including associated expenditure and timeframes ('project plan'), with each milestone consisting of objective, measurable technical and non-technical markers ('milestone descriptors') selected from a catalogue accessible on the Scheme portal. 8. The Central Government considers the project plan without modification or with such modifications as to timeframes, project cost, milestone descriptor, or milestone as it may deem fit and accords its in-principle approval to such submitted or modified project plan ('in-principle approved project plan'), or includes it as a waitlisted project, or rejects the same. 9. If in-principle approval is accorded with modifications, the communication conveying the same may specify such modifications or require the applicant to carry out changes in the project plan in line with directions contained in such communication and resubmit the plan. 10. Upon conveying acceptance and submission (to the satisfaction of the Central Government), the project plan or the modified project plan is referred to as the approved project plan. 11. Within a period of 60 days from the date of conveying acceptance and submissions, the applicant submits: (i) documents evidencing financial closure for the approved total project cost, (ii) duly signed funding agreement with the approved project plan annexed thereto, (iii) if the applicant has opted for benefit-share by way of share allotment, duly signed share allotment agreement, and (iv) if the Central Government has required a report to validate that the product or technology which the project proposed to take to higher TRLs is at TRL claimed in the application, such report furnished by an agency from among those specified on the Scheme portal for this purpose. 12. Failure to convey acceptance or make any submission as aforesaid within the specified period (or such extended period or periods as the Central Government may permit) renders the in-principle approval liable to be withdrawn. 13. A waitlisted project is retained in the waitlist for a period of 180 days from the date on which its waitlisting is communicated to the applicant, along with the approved project plan, for acceptance of the waitlisting within a period of 10 days of such communication. 14. Every beneficiary and waitlisted applicant is required to implement the approved project plan as per the milestone descriptors and associated expenditure and timeframe specified therein, in accordance with the provisions of the Scheme and these guidelines, unless the Central Government permits modification of the same in writing. 15. In case the Central Government permits any modification, the reference to 'approved project plan' is construed as a reference to the modified project plan. 16. In case of the share allotment option for benefit-share, an applicant company may either allot its own shares or the shares of a company incorporated by it as a dedicated, special purpose vehicle for purposes of implementation of the project and fulfilment of all obligations of the applicant/beneficiary under the Scheme and these guidelines. 17. Disbursement of milestone-linked financial assistance is done in the following manner: (a) Number of instalments: Disbursement of financial assistance is made in four instalments. The fourth instalment will be at least 15% of the approved total financial assistance and will be disbursed on a reimbursement basis. (b) Co-funding: Total co-funding will be required to the extent specified in the Scheme and would equal the approved total project cost less the approved total financial assistance, and the proportion of total co-funding to the approved total financial assistance is required to be maintained at the stage of disbursement of each instalment. Accordingly, the beneficiary is required to ensure that cumulative co-funding in said proportion to the sum of such instalment and financial assistance previously disbursed is credited into the project account before such disbursement. (c) Criteria for disbursement: On achievement of the milestones as per corresponding criteria, instalments as specified below will be disbursed, which may be utilised for the corresponding purposes: [Milestone, Criteria for achievement of milestone, Milestone-linked instalment to be disbursed, Purposes for which instalments disbursed may be utilised] Milestone 0 (M0): 1. For all applicants: 1.1 Evidencing financial closure for the approved total project cost, 1.2 Execution of funding agreement. 2. Further, for applicants selecting share allotment option of benefit-share: 2.1 Execution of share allotment agreement. 3. Also, for all projects for [details truncated due to length, but process is fully described in evidence].
required_documents: Application form prescribed in Appendix K
Undertaking in the format given in Appendix E
Undertaking in the format given in Appendix G consenting audit of manufacturing site/offices
Project plan based on sequential milestones to be achieved including associated expenditure and timeframes
Documents evidencing financial closure for the approved total project cost
Duly signed funding agreement with the approved project plan annexed thereto
If opting for benefit-share by way of share allotment: duly signed share allotment agreement

If required by Central Government: report to validate that the product or technology which the project proposed to take to higher TRLs is at TRL claimed in the application, furnished by an agency from among those specified on the Scheme portal

Valid contract executed between the applicant and Academia, or in case such contract has not yet been executed: academic intent letter signed by an officer of Academia duly authorised in this behalf, downloadable from the Scheme portal

Valuation report of the IP proposed to be in-licensed from Academia, furnished by an agency from among those specified for this purpose on the Scheme portal based on fair value assessment of such IP, or in case such assessment has not been done: estimate of the value of that IP in the project plan as referred to in paragraph 3.4.5 and in the aforesaid valid contract or academic intent letter
deadlines: The Scheme shall be open for applications during the Application Window. No application shall be accepted after the end of the Application Window.
implementing_agency: Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India
caveats: Total financial assistance to applicants belonging to a related group as referred to in paragraph 3.4.2 will not exceed 5% of the total financial outlay of the Scheme, unless the Central Government decides otherwise.
The Central Government reserves the right to amend the said Scheme and these guidelines from time to time.
Expressions used in these guidelines have the same meaning as in the said Scheme.

The applicant will disclose in its application all companies, LLPs and registered partnership firms belonging to its related group, regardless of whether such a company, LLP or registered partnership firm is an applicant under the Scheme or not.

Financial assistance will be disbursed into a project-specific dedicated account opened by the applicant in a Scheduled Commercial Bank prior to execution of the funding agreement ('project account'). Such account will be subject to inspection and special audit, including forensic audit, by the Central Government and will fulfil other requirements specified on the Scheme portal.

The Central Government will have the right to call for more information in respect of financial closure and take assistance from any instrumentality of the State or from parties from whom co-funding for financial closure is to be sourced, for the purpose of verifying any documents submitted to satisfy itself as to the veracity of the financial closure claimed and such documents.

A waitlisted project will be retained in the waitlist for a period of 180 days from the date on which its waitlisting is communicated to the applicant, along with the approved project plan, for acceptance of the waitlisting within a period of 10 days of such communication.

Every beneficiary and waitlisted applicant will be required to implement the approved project plan as per the milestone descriptors and associated expenditure and timeframe specified therein, in accordance with the provisions of the Scheme and these guidelines, unless the Central Government permits modification of the same in writing.

In case of the share allotment option for benefit-share, an applicant company may either allot its own shares, or the shares of a company incorporated by it as a dedicated, special purpose vehicle for purposes of implementation of the project and fulfilment of all obligations of the applicant/beneficiary under the Scheme and these guidelines.

Disbursement of milestone-linked financial assistance: Number of instalments: Disbursement of financial assistance will be made in four instalments. The fourth instalment will be at least 15% of the approved total financial assistance and will be disbursed on a reimbursement basis.

Co-funding: Total co-funding will be required to the extent specified in the Scheme and would equal the approved total project cost less the approved total financial assistance, and the proportion of total co-funding to the approved total financial assistance is required to be maintained at the stage of disbursement of each instalment.
Criteria for disbursement: On achievement of the milestones as per corresponding criteria, instalments as specified below will be disbursed, which may be utilised for the corresponding purposes.

In case of the share allotment option for benefit-share, an applicant company may either allot its own shares, or the shares of a company incorporated by it as a dedicated, special purpose vehicle for purposes of implementation of the project and fulfilment of all obligations of the applicant/beneficiary under the Scheme and these guidelines.
